text: 1g of fat. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0g of carbs. everything else here is pretty flat. in this category, carbs barely matter.
29g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 15% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
zinc at 13% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

pros first: high satiety protein source because that is a major real-world strength for meat. complete protein delivery is reliable because the category is supposed to do this well. easy to centre a meal around because practical meal value matters.

on the weaker side: fat quality can go sideways fast because some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up. cost and storage can be a pain because practical downsides still count. brings no fibre support because that leaves a nutrition gap the rest of the meal has to cover.
B tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
